channel.title: i2c on SoundsBrown
channel.description: Recent content in i2c on SoundsBrown
channel.copyright: This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International License.

item.title: Generating audio
item.description: Implementing a VCO using an Arduino is apparently trickier than one might expect. Using a DAC Neither I2C (e.g. MCP4725) nor SPI (e.g. MCP4922) are fast enough to generate audio-rate waveforms. Do not expect to achieve more than, say, 200 Hz. I2S I have thusfar been unable to produce any output with the I2S DAC I have and an MKRZERO. Perhaps try omitting the DAC and verify whether the I2S pins produce any signal at all.

item.title: Clock Source
item.description: Arduino-based quad clock pulse generator. The topmost knob sets the master tempo in BPM. The other three channels are derived from the master with a multiplier that can range from 1⁄16 to 16. The settings for each channel are shown on an I2C OLED display. Output for each channel is a 5V square wave with 50% duty cycle.

item.title: LFO
item.description: Arduino-based digital LFO. Rate from 0.01 Hz to 37 Hz using a non-linear scale. Six waveforms: saw, ramp, triangle, square, sine and value noise (Perlin-esque). PWM control for all waveforms. Unipolar output (0V - 5V) through an MCP4725 DAC (I2C interface, 12 bit resolution). Optional status display on an I2C OLED. Output is buffered using a TL08x References: https://www.sparkfun.com/products/12918 https://github.com/olikraus/U8g2_Arduino
